title: 続きを/voteで続けて。 • consensus mode の前提を確認します。まず Claude と GLM の live lane を起動できるかを確認し、同時に現在の auth- stale 導線設計を維持したまま continuation 可能かを見ます。 • Ran which glm └ glm not found • Explored └ Read agent-glm-general-reviewer.json, agent-claude-general-reviewer.json ────────────────────────────────────────────────...
summary_text: - **IA drift from philosophy:** docs say five operational views (`Happy/Now/Tasks/Alerts/Recover`), but UI nav is four (`Home/Work/Live/Recover`) with `Alerts` and `Tasks` collapsed into one `Work` surface; this weakens “single-front control surface” clarity for operators. - **Initial login/route clarity is partial:** `/happy/auth/` exists and `returnTo` restore works, but the main app still boots into `Home` and treats auth state as a status-strip condition, not a first-class entry state; the unused `describeAuthSurface()` path suggests intended but unwired auth UX. - **Concurrent task/lane topology is too shallow:** current UI shows counts plus top 3 cards and route tags, but no explicit lane map (provider/lane role, ownership, queue vs running per lane), so multi-lane Kernel state is not operator-auditable on mobile. - **Task-bound natural-language control is missing:** compose is global only; task sheet has outputs/events/recover but no per-task “follow-up instruction” input, so users cannot issue scoped directives against a selected running task. - **Cost/comms telemetry is designed but not productized:** `happy-control-surface.js` has telemetry/comms summaries (cost, tokens, provider mix, communication count), yet nothing in `app.js`/`index.html` renders it, and goal gate doesn’t enforce it. - **Smallest viable product boundary:** keep auth gateway + `Home` compose + `Work` queue/detail + bounded `Recover`, add only three missing pieces before widening scope: (1) explicit auth-state entry card, (2) task-scoped instruction action in detail sheet, (3) compact operator telemetry strip (provider mix/cost/comms).
